notes: These evals depend on live GitHub state. The key things being tested are classification accuracy, delegation routing, `.ai/work-my-backlog/` state persistence, current-iteration scoping, linked-PR rescans, and author-metadata-based comment classification.

evals.name: mixed-backlog-all-stages
evals.expected_output: Classifies assigned GitHub issues into Stage 1/2/3 and emits the corresponding `plan`, `revise_plan`, `implement`, and `babysit_pr` packets. Writes `scan-state.json`, `last-scan.json`, and per-item `wi-<id>.json` files under `.ai/work-my-backlog/`, then prints a summary covering scope, actionable items, skipped items, and errors.

assertions.name: actionable_packets_emitted
assertions.description: Eligible items produce action packets with the expected action names for their stage/sub-state

assertions.name: scanner_state_written
assertions.description: `.ai/work-my-backlog/scan-state.json`, `last-scan.json`, and relevant `wi-<id>.json` files are written as valid JSON

assertions.name: correct_delegation
assertions.description: `babysit_pr` packets route to `gh:gh-babysit-pr-worker`; `plan`, `revise_plan`, and `implement` packets route to `gh:gh-work-on`

assertions.name: summary_presented
assertions.description: The final summary reports scope plus actionable, skipped, and error sections

evals.name: second-pass-state-continuity
evals.precondition: `.ai/work-my-backlog/scan-state.json` and one or more `wi-<id>.json` files already exist from a previous pass
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing global/per-item state, cross-checks with live GitHub data, updates changed items, increments `passCount`, and preserves addressed thread IDs for Stage 3 packets.

assertions.name: state_loaded
assertions.description: Reads existing `scan-state.json` and `wi-<id>.json` files before deciding whether an item needs rescanning

assertions.name: pass_count_incremented
assertions.description: `passCount` in `scan-state.json` is previous + 1

assertions.name: stage_transition_detected
assertions.description: Work item that gained a PR since last pass is reclassified from Stage 2 to Stage 3

assertions.name: addressed_threads_preserved
assertions.description: Previously addressed thread IDs from state are passed to babysit-pr-worker

evals.name: stale-worktree-hint-cleared
evals.precondition: A tracked `wi-<id>.json` contains `worktreePath` and `branch`, but `git worktree list` no longer includes that path
evals.expected_output: Does not recreate the missing worktree. Instead, clears the stale `worktreePath`/`branch` fields in `wi-<id>.json` and continues scanning.

assertions.name: missing_worktree_cleared
assertions.description: Stale `worktreePath` and `branch` values are reset to null in the per-item state

assertions.name: no_worktree_creation_required
assertions.description: The scan succeeds without creating a new `.worktrees/wi-<id>/` directory

assertions.name: scan_continues_after_clear
assertions.description: The item can still be classified and included in actionable/skipped/error output after the stale hint is cleared

evals.name: linked-issues-scanned-independently
evals.precondition: 3 linked GitHub issues share parent/child or predecessor relationships, all are assigned/open, and all are currently Stage 1
evals.expected_output: Classifies the items independently from their own comments/PR state. The run does not require dependency-based ordering or blocking notes before producing `plan` packets.

assertions.name: independent_stage1_packets
assertions.description: Each eligible linked issue can appear as its own `plan` packet

assertions.name: no_dependency_sort_required
assertions.description: The run does not require predecessor-first or child-before-parent ordering to classify the items

assertions.name: no_dependency_blocking_summary
assertions.description: The summary reflects actual scan results without inventing dependency-blocking notes

evals.name: assigned-open-fallback
evals.prompt: work on everything assigned to me
evals.precondition: No current project iteration metadata is available, but the user has open assigned issues
evals.expected_output: Falls back to assigned open issues, finds and classifies items correctly.

assertions.name: fallback_query_used
assertions.description: When no current iteration is available, falls back to assigned open issues

assertions.name: items_found
assertions.description: Assigned open issues are found despite missing iteration metadata

evals.name: auto-mode-no-confirmation
evals.expected_output: Proceeds immediately after classification without asking for user confirmation. If actionable items are present, routes them autonomously.

assertions.name: no_confirmation_prompt
assertions.description: Skill does NOT ask 'should I proceed?' or wait for user input before processing

assertions.name: autonomous_processing
assertions.description: All stages are processed without pausing for user interaction

evals.name: unassigned-items-removed-from-state
evals.precondition: A `wi-<id>.json` exists from a previous pass, but that issue is now closed or no longer assigned/open and absent from the current assigned-work-items query
evals.expected_output: Removes the stale `wi-<id>.json` file during cleanup after the current scan. No separate `pr-<id>.json` cleanup or worktree removal step is required.

assertions.name: tracked_item_removed
assertions.description: Tracked per-item state is removed when the issue is no longer assigned/open

assertions.name: cleanup_does_not_block_scan
assertions.description: Cleanup completes without preventing `scan-state.json` and `last-scan.json` from being updated

evals.name: error-resilience
evals.precondition: 3 work items, one of which consistently fails (e.g., GitHub API error on fetch)
evals.expected_output: Failing item is recorded in `errors`, its `errorCount` in `wi-<id>.json` increments, other items process normally, and after 3 consecutive failures the item is returned as skipped with reason `error_cap`.

assertions.name: error_does_not_block_others
assertions.description: Other work items are processed despite one item failing

assertions.name: error_count_tracked
assertions.description: `errorCount` in the per-item state is incremented for the failing item

assertions.name: error_cap_after_third_failure
assertions.description: After 3 consecutive failures, the item is skipped with reason `error_cap`

evals.name: current-iteration-scoped
evals.precondition: Assigned open issues include a mix of current-iteration items and non-current backlog items in GitHub Projects
evals.expected_output: Scopes processing to the assigned issues in the current GitHub Project iteration and reports that iteration in the scan summary instead of processing the full assigned-open set.

assertions.name: current_iteration_only_processed
assertions.description: Only assigned issues in the current iteration are processed when current-iteration metadata is available

assertions.name: iteration_summary_matches_scope
assertions.description: The scan summary iteration label reflects the active current-iteration scope

evals.name: stage3-pr-activity-rescan
evals.precondition: A Stage 3 work item has unchanged issue.updatedAt since the last pass, but the linked open PR has new review/check activity or changed readiness/conflict state
evals.expected_output: Rescans the item from linked PR activity, and draft/conflicted PRs remain actionable instead of being skipped as healthy waiting items.

assertions.name: linked_pr_activity_triggers_rescan
assertions.description: Linked PR activity is sufficient to rescan a Stage 3 work item even when the issue timestamp is unchanged

assertions.name: draft_or_conflict_not_skipped
assertions.description: Draft or conflicted PRs are routed as actionable Stage 3 work instead of being skipped as waiting on humans or checks

evals.name: author-metadata-human-feedback
evals.precondition: An issue has BOT-PLAN comments and follow-up comments where bot and human authors are distinguishable through GitHub actor metadata, including at least one human comment mentioning '[bot]' in the body
evals.expected_output: Uses GitHub author metadata when available so bot-authored comments are ignored and human-authored feedback/approvals are still classified correctly even if the comment body contains bot-like markers.

assertions.name: author_metadata_preferred
assertions.description: Bot vs human classification prefers GitHub actor metadata over comment-body marker heuristics when metadata is available

assertions.name: human_feedback_retained
assertions.description: Human feedback is not discarded solely because the text contains '[bot]' or similar markers
